title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
summary: PostHog is building an agent-driven pipeline that automatically converts product signals like rage clicks, error spikes, and customer messages into pull requests. The system uses LLM-generated query embeddings to cluster related signals by semantic meaning, then routes them to agents that research the codebase and generate fixes.
writeup: Joshua Snyder of PostHog describes an autonomous system that turns product signals such as rage clicks, errors, and Slack messages into pull requests through a background agent. The agent groups related signals, researches the codebase, and opens PRs to fix the underlying issues, compressing the path from signal detection to resolution. The talk covers the supporting machinery, including embeddings for grouping, information retrieval over the codebase, error recovery, and prompt optimization.
